title: 10 best herbal teas for acne
intro_desc: Herbal teas for acne are infusions made from various plants and herbs that are believed to help reduce acne symptoms. These teas are caffeine-free and rich in antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals that can improve skin health. Some popular herbal teas for acne include chamomile, green tea, and dandelion. Chamomile tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe irritated skin and reduce redness. Green tea is rich in antioxidants that can help reduce inflammation and prevent acne breakouts. Dandelion tea can help improve liver function, which can lead to clearer skin. By incorporating these herbal teas into their daily routine, individuals can improve their skin health, reduce acne symptoms, and boost their overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow teas helps with acne because it contains a compound called chamazulene, which has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. This tea can help to reduce redness and swelling associated with acne by soothing irritated skin and preventing infection-causing bacteria from multiplying. Additionally, yarrow's natural antiseptic qualities can also help to purify the skin and unclog pores, allowing for a clearer complexion. By drinking this tea regularly, individuals may find an improvement in their acne symptoms over time.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Yarrow tea's antibacterial compounds, such as chamazulene and saponins, help combat Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria that cause acne, reducing inflammation and preventing new breakouts.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, including flavonoids and terpenes, reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with acne, allowing the skin to heal faster and more effectively.
Astringent and antiseptic properties: Yarrow tea's astringent and antiseptic compounds help dry up excess oil and reduce pore size, preventing clogged pores and reducing the appearance of acne scars.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help reduce redness and inflammation associated with acne.
Flowers: Containing a high concentration of flavonoids and terpenes, which have antibacterial properties that combat acne-causing bacteria.
remedy_recipe: Gather one tablespoon of dried achillea millefolium flowers for every eight ounces of water.
Measure one teaspoon of fresh achillea millefolium leaves or two teaspoons of dried leaves for every cup of water.
Heat the water in a teapot or kettle to boiling point before reducing it to a simmer.
Steep the herb mixture in the hot water for five to seven minutes depending on desired strength.
Strain and discard solids, then sweeten with honey or lemon to taste, serving immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold teas helps with acne because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which work together to reduce redness and swelling associated with breakouts. The antioxidants in pot marigold also help to neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and lead to clogged pores, thereby preventing the formation of acne lesions. Additionally, the tea's antimicrobial properties help to combat P. acnes bacteria that can cause acne, reducing inflammation and promoting a clear complexion.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Pot marigold tea contains calyx, which has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that can contribute to acne, reducing inflammation and preventing future breakouts.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea's flavonoids and phenolic acids have anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with acne, and promoting healing and tissue repair.

Antioxidant properties: Pot marigold tea is rich in antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals, which can damage skin cells and contribute to acne formation; antioxidants also promote overall skin health and resilience.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant are used to make teas that help reduce inflammation and redness associated with acne, due to their anti-inflammatory properties.

Flowers: The flowers of the pot marigold plant contain flavonoids, which have antioxidant and antibacterial properties, making them effective in fighting acne-causing bacteria and reducing oxidative stress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers or purchase dried flowers for best flavor and aroma.
Measure one teaspoon of dried calendula flowers or three teaspoons of fresh petals per 8 oz cup.
Heat filtered water to a boil in a teapot or kettle and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth to remove loose herbs.
Add honey or lemon to taste and enjoy warm, or refrigerate and serve chilled within 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea teas helps with acne because of its high antioxidant content, which can help reduce inflammation in the skin. The catechins present in white tea, particularly epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG), have been shown to inhibit the growth of Propionibacterium acnes bacteria, a common cause of acne. Additionally, white tea's antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties can help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation associated with breakouts. By incorporating white tea into your skincare routine, you may experience a reduction in acne severity and appearance.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: White tea is rich in antioxidants like catechins and flavonoids, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress that contribute to acne development.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal white teas like chamomile and calendula contain anti-inflammatory compounds like apigenin and luteolin, which may help reduce redness, swelling, and inflammation associated with acne.

Antimicrobial agents: Certain herbal white tea ingredients, such as peppermint and eucalyptus, possess antimicrobial properties that can help combat bacterial overgrowth on the skin, a common factor in acne development.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the white tea plant contain antioxidants and flavonoids that help reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth, making it effective in treating acne.
Bark: The bark of the white tea plant has astringent properties that can help dry out pimples and reduce their appearance.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh camellia sinensis leaves for the best flavor and aroma quality.
Rinse the harvested leaves with cold filtered water to remove any impurities.
Soak one teaspoon of dried white tea leaves or two teaspoons of fresh leaves in boiling water for 7-8 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove excess solids.
Serve and enjoy, as this herbal white tea is ready to drink immediately, with a hint of delicate flavor.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile teas helps with acne because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The apigenin in chamomile tea has been shown to reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, while its antiseptic compounds combat the bacteria that cause breakouts. Additionally, chamomile's soothing effects can help calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and promoting a clearer complexion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: German Chamomile tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like apigenin, luteolin, and chamazulene, which help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, making it easier to heal.
Antibacterial properties: The tea's antibacterial properties can help combat the bacteria that causes acne (P. acnes), reducing the formation of new pimples and preventing further inflammation.

Antioxidant activity: German Chamomile tea's antioxidant compounds neutralize free radicals, which can damage skin cells and contribute to acne development. This property helps protect the skin from oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation and soothe skin irritations associated with acne.
Flowers: Contain apigenin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that calms redness and swelling caused by acne.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried german chamomile flowers carefully measuring one teaspoon per eight ounces.
Rinse the flowers gently with cold water to remove any dirt or debris then drain well.
Combine the rinsed flowers with boiling water in a medium-sized heat-resistant glass teapot letting steep.
Allow the mixture to infuse for 5-7 minutes or until the desired strength and flavor are achieved then strain.
Pour the tea into a cup and enjoy immediately sweetening if desired or store in an airtight container refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chicory teas helps with acne because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce redness and swelling associated with breakouts. The tea's antioxidants also help to combat free radicals that can contribute to acne by damaging skin cells and causing inflammation. Additionally, chicory tea may help to regulate hormonal imbalances, which are a common underlying cause of acne. By soothing and balancing the skin, chicory tea can promote a clearer and healthier complexion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Chicory contains anti-inflammatory compounds such as sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne.

Antibacterial properties: The inulin and saponins present in chicory have been shown to exhibit antibacterial activity against Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), the bacteria that can contribute to the development of acne.
Antioxidant activity: Chicory tea's high antioxidant content, including vitamin C, E, and beta-carotene, helps neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and exacerbate acne.
remedy_parts: Roots: Known as a natural anti-inflammatory, chicory roots help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne.
Rhyzomes: With its antimicrobial properties, chicory rhyzomes can help combat bacteria-causing acne and promote healthy skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of dried chicory root or 4-6 fresh chicory roots for every liter of water.
Clean and chop the chicory root into small pieces to ensure optimal infusion.
Boil one liter of water in a pot and add the chopped chicory root for steeping time of 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids and discard the roots.
Serve the tea hot or let it cool to room temperature and store in an airtight container for up to two days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eastern purple coneflower teas helps with acne because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The tea's active compounds, including kaempferol and quercetin, have been shown to reduce redness and swelling associated with acne-prone skin, while also inhibiting the growth of Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria that can clog pores. Additionally, the tea's antioxidants help to soothe and calm the skin, promoting a clearer complexion and reducing the appearance of blemishes.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial compounds: Eastern Purple Coneflower tea contains antibacterial compounds such as triterpenoids and phenolic acids, which can help combat bacteria like Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) that contribute to the development of acne.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, including flavonoids and terpenes, can reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, while also soothing and calming the skin.
Antioxidant activity: Eastern Purple Coneflower tea is rich in antioxidants, which help neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to the development of acne.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used for their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce acne-causing bacteria and soothe skin irritations.
Buds: The buds are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and prevent oxidative damage that can contribute to acne.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried echinacea purpurea flowers in the morning for optimal potency and aroma.
Measure two to three teaspoons of dried flowers or four to six teaspoons of fresh flowers per eight ounce cup.
Heat filtered water to a temperature between 195°f and 205°f and steep the herbs for seven to ten minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids before serving.
Store any leftover tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horsetail teas helps with acne because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling associated with breakouts. The tea's high silica content also plays a crucial role in regulating skin cell turnover, promoting healthy collagen production, and tightening pores to prevent clogged skin. Additionally, horsetail's antimicrobial properties help combat bacteria that can cause acne, further reducing the risk of inflammation and blemishes.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Horsetail tea's antioxidants help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to acne development and make skin more susceptible to damage.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in horsetail tea may help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with acne, promoting a clearer complexion.
Sodium content: Horsetail tea is rich in sodium, which can help regulate hormonal imbalances that can contribute to acne, particularly for women with polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS).
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of horsetail contain high amounts of silica, which can help to reduce inflammation and improve skin clarity.
Stems: The stems of horsetail are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which can help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation caused by acne.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh horsetail leaves or stems in early morning or evening to preserve their potency and flavor.
Rinse the harvested plant material gently with cold running water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine 10-20 grams of dried or 30-60 grams of fresh horsetail leaves or stems with 500ml boiling water in a large pot.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes, then strain it through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve to remove solids and release essence.
Allow the tea to cool to room temperature before serving and consuming within 24 hours of preparation for optimal benefits.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel teas helps with acne because of its potent antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's active compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, work to reduce inflammation in the skin, which can help to alleviate acne symptoms like redness and swelling. Additionally, fennel's antibacterial properties help to combat Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), a bacteria that contributes to clogged pores and acne breakouts. By reducing inflammation and killing P. acnes, fennel tea may help to clear up acne-prone skin and promote healthier, clearer complexions.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Fennel tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and polyphenols, which can reduce inflammation in the skin and alleviate acne-related redness and swelling.

Antibacterial properties: Fennel's essential oil has antibacterial properties that can help combat Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), a bacteria that contributes to the development of acne by clogging pores and causing inflammation.
Antioxidant activity: Fennel tea is rich in antioxidants, which can neutralize free radicals that damage skin cells and contribute to acne development.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, fennel roots help reduce inflammation and promote healthy skin.
Leaves: Fennel leaves are known for their astringent properties, which help dry up pimples and reduce redness, making them an effective ingredient in acne-fighting teas.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried fennel seeds foeniculum vulgare for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Heat the water in a saucepan to a rolling boil and remove from heat.
Add the measured fennel seeds to the hot water and let steep for five to seven minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Enjoy the warm herbal tea immediately or let it cool to room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with acne because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling associated with breakouts. The tea's flavonoids and isoflavones also exhibit antibacterial effects, inhibiting the growth of Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria that can clog pores and cause acne. Additionally, licorice root's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and exacerbate acne. By addressing these underlying factors, herbal licorice tea may contribute to a clearer, healthier complexion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Licorice root contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glabridin and glabrillin, which can help reduce inflammation in the skin and alleviate redness and swelling associated with acne.

Antioxidants: Herbal licorice tea is rich in antioxidants that can help neutralize free radicals and protect the skin from damage caused by environmental stressors, pollution, and UV radiation, all of which can contribute to acne development.

Hormone-balancing properties: Licorice root has been shown to have hormone-balancing effects, particularly in relation to cortisol and adrenaline levels, which can help regulate sebum production and reduce acne-causing hormonal imbalances.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps reduce inflammation and soothe skin irritations associated with acne.
Bark: The bark contains triterpenoids, which have antibacterial properties that can help combat Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria that contribute to acne.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and chop them into small pieces to release their active compounds.
Combine one teaspoon of chopped licorice root with one cup of boiling water in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes, depending on personal preference for flavor intensity, before straining and discarding solids.
Add honey or other sweeteners if desired, as licorice root has a naturally sweet flavor, and enjoy the warm tea immediately.
Store any leftover tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours without refrigeration for future consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sea buckthorn teas helps with acne because it is rich in antioxidants, omega-7 fatty acids, and other beneficial compounds that can help soothe and calm the skin. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties may reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, while its antibacterial properties can help combat P. acnes bacteria, which can cause breakouts. Additionally, sea buckthorn tea's moisturizing effects may help to balance the skin's natural pH levels, reducing excess oil production and clogged pores that often lead to acne.
remedy_properties: Rich in Antioxidants: Sea buckthorn tea is rich in antioxidants, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress caused by acne-causing bacteria, leading to a reduction in acne severity.

Antibacterial Properties: The tea contains antimicrobial compounds that inhibit the growth of Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), a type of bacteria that contributes to acne, thereby reducing the occurrence of pimples and inflammation.
Anti-Inflammatory Effects: Sea buckthorn tea's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with acne, promoting faster healing and reduced scarring.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sea buckthorn roots are rich in flavonoids and saponins, which help reduce inflammation and combat bacteria that cause acne.
Seeds: The seeds of the sea buckthorn plant contain fatty acids and vitamins A and E, which can help soothe and calm the skin, reducing acne symptoms.
Bark: Sea buckthorn bark has been traditionally used to treat skin conditions like acne due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried sea buckthorn leaves and flowers, ensuring sufficient quantity for brewing.
Measure out two teaspoons of dried sea buckthorn mix or one tablespoon of loose-leaf tea per eight ounces water.
Heat filtered water to a temperature between 195°f and 205°f, allowing it to cool slightly before pouring.
Steep the sea buckthorn mixture in hot water for ten to fifteen minutes, depending on desired strength and flavor.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding solids and serving immediately or storing refrigerated for up to three days.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat acne with herbal teas is by regularly drinking chamomile tea, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and swelling. Another effective herbal tea is green tea, rich in antioxidants that combat acne-causing bacteria and reduce excess oil production. Additionally, spearmint tea can help regulate hormonal imbalances that often trigger acne. Regularly consuming these herbal teas, along with a balanced diet and skincare routine, can significantly improve acne-prone skin.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to acne that are treated with herbal teas include eczema, dermatitis, and psoriasis. Chamomile tea is known for its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe irritated skin and reduce redness. Burdock root tea is often used as a blood purifier, helping to eliminate toxins from the body that can cause skin issues. Nettle leaf tea has antihistamine properties that can help reduce inflammation and itching. Lastly, calendula tea is known for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, making it a great option for treating skin irritations and infections. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new herbal remedies.
